evals.prompt: Walk me through the complete customer onboarding journey from when they first visit freyaivisions.de to when they're ready for a quote. Include all steps, fields, and data flows.
evals.expected_output: Complete 3-layer journey: Auth → Setup Wizard (3 steps, 13 fields) → Fragebogen (52 questions) → Tier recommendation → Angebot.

assertions.name: auth-layer
assertions.description: Describes authentication as first layer (email, OAuth, or magic link)

assertions.name: setup-wizard-3-steps
assertions.description: Mentions setup wizard with 3 steps

assertions.name: wizard-fields-count
assertions.description: Mentions 13 fields or lists fields from all 3 steps

assertions.name: fragebogen-mentioned
assertions.description: Mentions Fragebogen or questionnaire as a layer

assertions.name: fragebogen-52-questions
assertions.description: Mentions 52 questions or 11 sections

assertions.name: tier-recommendation
assertions.description: Mentions tier recommendation (Starter/Professional/Enterprise)

assertions.name: angebot-creation
assertions.description: Mentions Angebot or quote creation as outcome

assertions.name: data-persisted
assertions.description: Mentions data saving to profiles or Supabase

assertions.name: dsgvo-consent
assertions.description: Mentions DSGVO or Datenschutz consent

assertions.name: sequential-flow
assertions.description: Presents steps in correct sequential order

evals.prompt: I have a new customer who wants to import their existing customer list from Excel. How does the import work and what fields can they map?
evals.expected_output: 4-step Excel import wizard with column mapping, validation, and supported field list.

assertions.name: four-step-wizard
assertions.description: Describes 4 steps (upload, map, preview/validate, import)

assertions.name: xlsx-csv-supported
assertions.description: Mentions .xlsx or .csv file formats

assertions.name: column-mapping
assertions.description: Describes column mapping step (matching source to target fields)

assertions.name: validation-preview
assertions.description: Mentions validation or preview of data before import

assertions.name: supported-fields
assertions.description: Lists supported fields (firmenname, email, telefon, etc.)

assertions.name: email-dedup
assertions.description: Mentions deduplication on email or duplicate handling

assertions.name: umlaut-handling
assertions.description: Mentions UTF-8 encoding or German umlauts (ä, ö, ü)

assertions.name: kunden-table
assertions.description: Mentions kunden table as import target

assertions.name: error-handling
assertions.description: Mentions error rows or failed import handling

evals.prompt: How does the Fragebogen import service map questionnaire responses to system configuration? I want to understand how answers determine the recommended tier and which features get enabled.
evals.expected_output: Field mapping from fragebogen responses to profiles, settings, and tier recommendation logic with concrete Starter/Professional/Enterprise criteria.

assertions.name: import-service-referenced
assertions.description: References fragebogen-import-service.js

assertions.name: field-mappings
assertions.description: Describes specific field mappings (questionnaire field → system field)

assertions.name: tier-recommendation-logic
assertions.description: Explains tier recommendation based on employees, features, or budget

assertions.name: starter-criteria
assertions.description: Mentions Starter tier criteria: 1-3 employees, under €300k revenue, or basic modules only

assertions.name: professional-criteria
assertions.description: Mentions Professional tier criteria: 4-7 employees, €300k-€1M revenue, or automation modules

assertions.name: enterprise-criteria
assertions.description: Mentions Enterprise criteria: 8+ employees, over €1M revenue, or custom integrations

assertions.name: feature-toggles
assertions.description: Mentions feature toggles or settings being set from responses

assertions.name: datev-mapping
assertions.description: Mentions DATEV integration toggle from Section 9 (Buchhaltung) — datev_nutzung field

assertions.name: profiles-target
assertions.description: Mentions profiles table as data target

assertions.name: angebot-positions
assertions.description: Mentions matched positions or Angebot generation from responses

evals.prompt: A new customer completes the Fragebogen with: 5 employees, €450.000 Jahresumsatz, uses DATEV, and selected WhatsApp integration as a priority. Which tier do they get and what features are enabled?
evals.expected_output: Professional tier, DATEV-Export enabled, WhatsApp-Integration enabled, with correct pricing.

binary_assertions.name: professional-tier-assigned
binary_assertions.description: Correctly identifies Professional tier for 5 employees + €450k revenue

binary_assertions.name: datev-enabled
binary_assertions.description: States DATEV-Export module is enabled based on datev_nutzung = true

binary_assertions.name: whatsapp-enabled
binary_assertions.description: States WhatsApp-Integration is enabled based on priority selection

binary_assertions.name: angebot-positions-generated
binary_assertions.description: Mentions that draft Angebot positions are generated for enabled features

evals.prompt: What are the DSGVO requirements in the onboarding flow and where exactly do they appear?
evals.expected_output: DSGVO consent at auth (signup), data storage disclosure, and Abmeldemöglichkeit in all outbound emails.

binary_assertions.name: consent-at-signup
binary_assertions.description: Identifies mandatory DSGVO consent checkbox at account creation (auth.html)

binary_assertions.name: supabase-storage-mentioned
binary_assertions.description: Notes that data is stored in Supabase (profiles / fragebogen_responses tables)

binary_assertions.name: portal-token-privacy
binary_assertions.description: Notes that Fragebogen links use portal tokens, not raw user IDs

binary_assertions.name: admin-notification
binary_assertions.description: Notes admin (Jonas) is notified on Fragebogen completion

binary_assertions.name: data-minimization
binary_assertions.description: Notes that optional fields are truly optional — no forced collection

mutations_applied: Added full Tier Recommendation section with concrete Starter/Professional/Enterprise criteria (employees, revenue, budget thresholds)
Added DATEV toggle mapping: Section 9 datev_nutzung field → featureToggles.datevExport
Added WhatsApp, Calendar, Inventory toggle mappings from Fragebogen sections
Added Angebot position generation detail (each feature module → 1 line item)
Added DSGVO consent rule to auth layer explicitly
Added IndexedDB + Supabase persistence note to Wizard Rules
